topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The string quartet swelled, a lush, familiar sound that suddenly felt jarring against the sudden, sharp intake of my breath. I was standing near the canapés, nursing a lukewarm sparkling water, when I saw him. Tall, impossibly well-dressed, laughing with a group near the entrance. My fork clattered onto the linen tablecloth, the small sound echoing loudly in the sudden vacuum of my attention. Every muscle in my jaw tightened. I forced myself to look away, focusing intently on the intricate pattern of the wallpaper, trying to anchor myself to the present moment, but my vision kept snagging on the familiar silhouette. When he turned, our eyes met across the crowded ballroom. A strange, hollow space opened in my chest, and I felt an urgent, almost physical need to melt into the nearest potted palm.

[story 2]

"Oh, wow, look who it is!" Maya’s voice cut through the ambient chatter, and I followed her gaze. There he was, leaning against a marble pillar, looking entirely too relaxed, a crooked smile playing on his lips. My carefully constructed composure fractured instantly. My hands, which had been loosely holding my champagne flute, began to tremble almost imperceptibly. I felt a sudden, dizzying lurch in my stomach, like the floor had tilted. I took a slow, deliberate step backward, bumping lightly into a passing waiter, needing space, needing distance. The air suddenly felt thin, and the music seemed to drop out, leaving only a high-pitched ringing in my ears.

[story 3]

I was trying to discreetly check my phone when I realized I was staring. Then I saw him. Across the room, bathed in the warm glow of a nearby chandelier, he was talking animatedly to someone, his posture effortlessly commanding. A cold wave washed over me, chilling me right down to the bone despite the warmth of the room. My throat constricted; I could feel a dryness building up that made swallowing difficult. I gripped the edge of the railing I was leaning against, my knuckles turning white. I watched him for a long moment, cataloging every subtle shift in his expression, before abruptly pivoting, walking toward the exit without acknowledging anyone, needing to escape the gravitational pull of that shared history.
